jurisdiction: Telangana ULBs outside GHMC - municipal corporations and municipalities (Warangal/GWMC, Nizamabad, Karimnagar, Khammam, Ramagundam, Mahabubnagar, Siddipet, etc.)
regime: Telangana Building Rules — G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (07-04-2012) as amended, applied statewide to ULBs; TG-bPASS (TS-bPASS Act 2020) processing

instantApproval.note: Same statewide TG-bPASS instant registration tier as GHMC: plots up to 75 sq yd (about 62.71 m2) and height up to 7 m (G+1) - Re.1 token registration on self-declaration; 75 sq yd to 500 m2 / up to 10 m goes via instant self-certification approval.

source.ref: TS-bPASS Act 2020 / TG-bPASS portal guidance (tsbpass.telangana.gov.in); docs/region-research.md - TG section

setbacks.note: Same statewide G.O.Ms.No.168 family as tg-ghmc; kept identical to fixtures/code_rules.json TG. Sub-50 m2 no-setback relief exists in the rules but is not modeled.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (2012) residential setback table; docs/region-research.md - TG setback note

setbacks.note: 101-200 m2 relaxed band: front 1.5 m, sides and rear 1.0 m (road under 12 m).

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (2012) residential setback table; docs/region-research.md - TG setback note

setbacks.note: 201-300 m2 band on a road under 12 m. Road-width-scaled front setbacks are not modeled (see tg-ghmc note).

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (2012) residential setback table; docs/region-research.md - TG setback note
fixtures/code_rules.json TG setbacks (calibrated engine baseline); docs/region-research.md - TG section

setbacks.note: Baseline-engine extrapolation for large plots; the rules prescribe height-dependent setbacks for taller buildings.

source.ref: fixtures/code_rules.json TG setbacks (calibrated engine baseline); docs/region-research.md - TG section
G.O.Ms.No.168 height-vs-road-width provisions; docs/region-research.md - TG caveats (10 m on a road under 9 m)

heightByRoad.note: Simplified for smaller ULBs: 15 m cap on roads of 9 m and wider. Taller non-high-rise permissions on wide roads exist in some corporation areas per their master plans, but that granularity is not supportable statewide - treat wide-road / taller proposals as needing a jurisdiction-specific check.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 height-vs-road-width provisions; docs/region-research.md - TG caveats

far.note: Same setback-based control regime as tg-ghmc: no separate FAR cap for non-high-rise residential under the Telangana Building Rules 2012. The legacy engine working default is 1.5 (fixtures/code_rules.json TG.FAR) - advisory only.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 MA&UD (2012) - setback-based control regime; docs/region-research.md - TG confidence note

coverage.note: Practitioner working default consistent with fixtures/code_rules.json TG.maxGroundCoveragePct; not a printed statutory ceiling for individual houses.

parking.note: One covered car space plus two-wheeler space per dwelling as working default (stilt/ground parking; stilt up to 2.4 m clear exempt from height reckoning). ECS-based norms apply mainly to group housing.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 parking provisions (practitioner reading); docs/region-research.md - TG parking row

rwh.note: Rainwater-harvesting structure required for plots of 200 m2 and above (WALTA-heritage requirement continued in Telangana; enforced by ULBs as a sanction condition).

cornerPlot.note: Corner plots treat the second road-abutting side as a front setback (matched band's frontM applies on that flank). Junction splay may additionally be required.

source.ref: G.O.Ms.No.168 - corner plot treatment (common sanctioning practice)

roomMinimums.note: NBC-derived room minimums come from fixtures/code_rules.json states.TG block.

docChecklist: Latest property tax / vacant land tax receipt
Approved layout copy or LRS (layout regularisation) proceedings, where applicable
NALA conversion certificate (if the land was agricultural)
ULB master plan land-use extract / zoning confirmation for the plot
Building plans signed by the owner and a licensed technical person (LTP)
Structural stability certificate / soil report, as applicable to the proposed height
Mortgage deed for 10% of built-up area in favour of the ULB, where applicable
Rainwater-harvesting undertaking (plots of 200 m2 and above)
notes: Advisory defaults for early design only - NOT legal advice and NOT a substitute for a sanctioned plan. Verify against the live TG-bPASS engine and the current G.O.Ms.No.168 tables for the specific plot.

ULB-specific master plans / zonal development plans (e.g. Warangal Kakatiya UDA area, Nizamabad UDA) may OVERRIDE these statewide defaults - land use, road-widening lines and locally notified setback/height variations prevail over this pack.

Mortgage clause: 10% of built-up area mortgaged to the sanctioning ULB (released on occupancy certificate), commonly applied to plots above 200 m2 - threshold and mechanics NEED VERIFICATION against G.O.Ms.No.168 / TS-bPASS rules.
Same regime family as tg-ghmc with marginally simpler height handling; numbers for plots <= 500 m2 restate fixtures/code_rules.json TG values verbatim.
